title: ws.v1 — the public live WebSocket multiplex (/ws), frozen to vexa main
description: The /ws endpoint api-gateway serves: api_key auth (x-api-key header or ?api_key=), a subscribe/unsubscribe control protocol, and forwarded live data messages — transcript (the per-speaker confirmed/pending bundle) + transcription_segment from tc:meeting:{id}:mutable, meeting.status from bm:meeting:{id}:status, chat_message from va:meeting:{id}:chat. The gateway forwards the raw redis payload verbatim, so data messages are type-tagged and additive (extra fields allowed). Shapes are the canonical 0.10.6 contract (vexa-0.11 api-gateway + meetings.publish_meeting_status_change).

SubscribeRequest.description: client → server: subscribe to live updates for meetings

UnsubscribeRequest.description: client → server: stop receiving updates for meetings

Subscribed.description: server → client: ack of a subscribe (the meetings actually authorized + subscribed)

Unsubscribed.description: server → client: ack of an unsubscribe

TranscriptionSegment.description: server → client: a single live transcript segment (forwarded from tc:meeting:{id}:mutable). type + text are the floor; the producer adds speaker/timing/segment_id.

Transcript.description: server → client: the canonical 0.10.6 per-speaker transcript bundle (forwarded from tc:meeting:{id}:mutable) — `confirmed` (completed) + `pending` (in-progress) segment arrays for one speaker tick. This is what the collector publishes + the dashboard consumes live.

MeetingStatus.description: server → client: the canonical 0.10.6 meeting/bot status update (forwarded verbatim from bm:meeting:{id}:status). Status lives under `payload.status`; a `meeting` ref + `user_id` + `ts` are carried alongside (vexa-0.11 meetings.publish_meeting_status_change). Replaces the drifted `bot_status` frame.

ChatMessage.description: server → client: a chat message from the bot (forwarded from va:meeting:{id}:chat)

Error.description: server → client: a protocol/auth error. `error` is a fixed code from the vocabulary below (formalized as the enum on the property, so the conformance harness's emitted codes are machine-checked against this contract). Control/auth codes (exercised by the offline /ws conformance harness): missing_api_key, invalid_json, invalid_subscribe_payload, invalid_unsubscribe_payload, unknown_action. Downstream-auth codes — emitted by the deployed gateway's POST /ws/authorize-subscribe path (adapters.AdminApiAuthorizer), so they are PRODUCTION-ONLY and NOT reachable by the synchronous offline harness: authorization_service_error (downstream returned non-200; carries `status` + `detail`), authorization_call_failed (the downstream call raised; carries `details`).
